nyheter.title: Blue Water Autonomy, Saildrone Sue Navy Over MUSV Rejection
nyheter.summary: Two companies that were passed over for the U.S. Navy’s Medium Unmanned Surface Vessel program are taking the service to court over the decision, USNI News has learned. Saildrone and Blue Water Autonomy filed suit in late June in the United States Court of Federal Claims in Washington, D.C., with two separate complaints alleging the Navy didn’t follow its own rules in how it selected contenders for the first generation of larger autonomous vessels. In May, the Navy tapped seven out of about two

nyheter.title: Cahill Takes Command of U.S. Naval Surface Force
nyheter.summary: Vice Adm. Joe Cahill took over as the Navy’s top surface warfare officer earlier this month, USNI News has learned. Cahill, who most recently was the commander of Naval Surface Force Atlantic, took the helm of U.S. Naval Surface Force on July 3, according to a service news release. Cahill succeeds Vice Adm. Brendan McLane, who is retiring after serving as the SWOBOSS since December 2023. The ceremony occurred over the Fourth of July holiday weekend at Naval Amphibious Base Coronado in California

nyheter.title: Taiwan Mobilizes HIMARS, Anti-ship Missiles For Major Defense Drill
nyheter.summary: Taiwan’s most capable missile launchers are being deployed across the island for a five-day defense drill designed to enhance joint operations and develop concepts for targeting enemy aircraft and vessels. Newly procured American M142 High Mobility Rocket Artillery Systems (HIMARS) and domestically-produced Hsiung Feng anti-ship missile launchers will be used this week in simulated scenarios that involve detecting enemy maritime incursions into territorial waters, according to a Military News Ag

nyheter.title: First the coast guard, then an ICBM: China tests new, long-term ways to hold off rivals in Asia
nyheter.summary: Experts say Beijing used both methods to try out new, attention-grabbing and long-term ways to deter others from challenging its territorial claims.

nyheter.summary: The U.S. conducted a new wave of strikes against Iranian military sites on Wednesday after reimposing a naval blockade of the Islamic Republic's ports.

nyheter.summary: The new satellites for missile warning, tracking and targeting are being developed under an “accelerated” schedule to meet the Pentagon’s 2028 plans for demonstrating its Golden Dome missile defense shield.

nyheter.title: MARAD Ship Suffers Fire in Baltimore
nyheter.summary: Two lifeboats aboard U.S. Maritime Administration’s Charles L. Gilliland caught fire Monday in the Port of Baltimore. Baltimore City Fire Department responded around 11 a.m. to the two-alarm fire, meaning additional resources were needed to control the flames. There were no injuries reported. The cause of the fire is under investigation. Gilliland is one of MARAD’s roll-on, roll-off logistics ships. nyheter.title: Navy awards TOTE $2.2 billion to manage Medium Landing Ships project
nyheter.summary: The Navy awarded TOTE $2.2 billion to manage its medium landing ship construction efforts, with the first vessel scheduled for delivery in fall 2029.

nyheter.title: USS Essex at the Center of Additive Manufacturing Trials During RIMPAC 26
nyheter.summary: USS Essex (LHD-2) is the centerpiece of large-scale additive manufacturing tests during Rim of the Pacific 2026 exercise, as the U.S. Navy and Marine Corps lean on modern machine-made parts to hone self-repair capabilities. RIMPAC 26, which is being held in Hawaii through July 31, brings together military, government, academic, commercial and coalition partners in what the U.S. Navy has described as the largest advanced manufacturing demonstration conducted by the Defense Department. nyheter.title: Carrier USS Gerald R. Ford Starts First Major Post-Deployment Maintenance Period
nyheter.summary: Aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) is now at Norfolk Naval Shipyard for a maintenance period following a nearly 11-month deployment. Ford, the Navy’s newest in-service carrier, arrived last week at Norfolk Naval Shipyard in Portsmouth, Va., for its planned incremental availability, or PIA, according to a Navy news release. “[T]his marks Gerald R. nyheter.summary:
The Ukrainian armed forces have reportedly conducted a groundbreaking amphibious raid against Russian-held positions at the Kinburn Split. Executed entirely by unmanned vehicles operating across the sea, land, and air domains, the operation marks the first completely automated amphibious assault in military history conducted under wartime conditions. nyheter.title: Navy Awards TOTE $2.2B to Serve as Vessel Construction Manager for Landing Ship Medium
nyheter.summary: The Navy on Monday issued TOTE Services a $2.2 billion contract to function as the vessel construction manager for the Marine Corps’ Landing Ship Medium program, according to a Defense Department contract announcement. The award is worth up to $2.6 billion with options for as many as eight McClung-class LSMs. Gulf Coast-based Bollinger Shipyards is building the first LSM, while Wisconsin-based Fincantieri Marinette Marine in April won a $30 million for long lead items for four LSMs. “For t

nyheter.title: U.S. Reinstates Naval Blockade in Strait of Hormuz
nyheter.summary: The U.S. will resume its blockade of Iranian ports, coasts and ships, according to a Monday announcement from U.S. Central Command, signaling an end to the 60-day ceasefire between Iran and Washington. The U.S. lifted its blockade on June 18 as part of the memorandum of understanding between the two countries. The blockade will be reimplemented on Tuesday – less than one month into the ceasefire. nyheter.title: New Navy Policy Limits Medical Shaving Waivers to One Year
nyheter.summary: Sailors who cannot shave due to medical reasons now have one year before they could face separation over their inability to go clean shaven, according to a Navy policy released Tuesday. Under the most recent Navy administrative message, the sea service will offer shaving waivers for men who are unable to shave due to a medical condition. The waivers can be granted for 90 days, while commands have the option to offer additional 90-day waivers. The waivers cannot exceed one year total, according t

nyheter.title: USS Nimitz Arrives in Norfolk for Homeport Shift
nyheter.summary: The Navy’s oldest in-service aircraft carrier pulled into Norfolk, Va., on Thursday ahead of its final homeport shift, according to ship spotters. USS Nimitz (CVN-68) reached Virginia after participating in the International Naval Review in New York City over the Fourth of July, which coincided with the nation’s 250th birthday.
